name: Rank resumes by job description similarity with Gemini embeddings

parameters.formTitle: Sort Resumes by Job Description Similarity
parameters.formDescription: Upload resume PDFs and paste the job description. The workflow will return a CSV-formatted ranking of resumes by semantic similarity.

parameters.content:
## **Rank resumes by job description similarity with Gemini embeddings**

This workflow sorts uploaded resume PDFs by semantic similarity to a Job Description using Google Gemini multimodal embeddings.

Unlike text-extraction workflows, this version does not extract resume text first and does not use a separate OCR branch. It converts each uploaded PDF into base64, sends the PDF content directly to Gemini Embedding 2, compares each resume vector against the Job Description vector with cosine similarity, and returns a ranked list of filenames.

It is useful for recruiters, HR teams, and hiring teams who want a simple PDF-to-vector resume sorter before manual review.

## **Common use cases**

- Sort resume PDFs against one Job Description
- Compare text-based and scanned/image-based PDFs with one simpler flow
- Prioritize manual resume review
- Build a lightweight Gemini-native resume sorting workflow
- Avoid LLM-based candidate scoring or explanation generation

## **How it works**

- User uploads resume PDFs and pastes a Job Description
- The Job Description is embedded with `gemini-embedding-2`
- Each resume PDF is converted into base64
- Each PDF is sent directly to Gemini multimodal embeddings
- Cosine similarity compares each resume vector to the Job Description vector
- The workflow returns filenames sorted by similarity

## **Setup steps**

- Get a free Gemini API key from [Google AI Studio](https://aistudio.google.com/api-keys)
- Create or connect a Google Gemini credential in n8n
- Use that credential on:
  - **Create Job Description Embedding**
  - **Create Resume Embedding**
- Make sure your Google account/API key can use `gemini-embedding-2`
- Upload one or more resume PDFs
- Paste the Job Description
- Run the workflow
- Review the sorted filename list

## **Notes**

This workflow is a sorter, not an automated hiring decision system. It does not accept, reject, explain, or score candidates for the user. Similarity values are used internally for sorting.

The final output shows only:
- Rank
- File Name

## **Need Help?**
Have questions or want to connect? Reach me on [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/athaahsan/).

## 1. Upload resumes and embed the Job Description

The form collects:
- One or more resume PDFs
- One Job Description text

Only one credential is required for this workflow: **Google Gemini**.

You can get a free Gemini API key from [Google AI Studio](https://aistudio.google.com/api-keys).

Set the Google Gemini credential on:
- **Create Job Description Embedding**
- **Create Resume Embedding**

The Job Description is embedded once using `gemini-embedding-2`.

The Split Resume PDFs node turns uploaded files into separate resume items while preserving the original filename.

## 2. Convert each PDF directly into an embedding

Each uploaded resume PDF is converted into a base64 string.

The base64 PDF content is then sent directly to Gemini Embedding 2 as `inline_data` with MIME type `application/pdf`.

This version does not use:
- PDF text extraction
- OCR branching
- resume summarization
- LLM candidate review

Gemini creates one embedding vector for each resume PDF.

## 3. Compare vectors and return sorted filenames

The Code node compares:
- Job Description embedding
- Each resume PDF embedding

It uses cosine similarity to sort resumes from most similar to least similar.

The final result only shows:
- Rank
- File Name

The similarity score is used internally for sorting and is not shown in the final form response.
